purpose: Provision and release the canonical MMG subscription-commerce stack through an approval-gated, idempotent, observable, rollback-aware deployment control plane without exposing Shopify, database, customer, or storage credentials.

shopify_contract.publication_rule: Keep the product DRAFT until all end-to-end checks pass, then activate and publish only to the verified Online Store publication through the separate publish action.

rollback_contract.product_rollback: Return the product to DRAFT or unpublish it; do not delete customer contracts or delivered ownership.
rollback_contract.webhook_rollback: Release a prior app configuration version or disable the affected route after preserving the webhook inbox.
rollback_contract.database_rollback: Use forward repair migrations for applied commerce schemas unless a reviewed migration-specific rollback is approved.
rollback_contract.customer_rights: Never revoke delivered ownership solely because a deployment is rolled back.

privacy_and_security_contract: Never commit Shopify app secrets, Admin API tokens, database credentials, CSRF secrets, storage signing keys, or dispatcher secrets.
Authenticate the deployment endpoint with a server-to-server credential and require the deployment role.
Require an additional production release-manager role for production actions.
Do not accept live publication as part of general provisioning; publication is a separate approved action.
Persist runtime GIDs and hashed test references, but never persist raw access tokens, checkout tokens, test customer references, or raw test order identifiers.
Use idempotent request IDs and optimistic release versions for every action.
Preserve immutable audit events for every phase transition and failure.
release_gates: The Shopify app has protected subscription API access and every required scope.
Migrations 001 through 007 are applied in order to production PostgreSQL.
All required runtime routes are deployed and private routes enforce authentication, same-origin, CSRF, or HMAC as specified by their contracts.
The canonical product is provisioned as DRAFT with exact variants, prices, SKUs, nonshipping flags, metafields, and one shared monthly selling plan.
At least two digital assets pass subscription selection, media, package, secure-file, and recommendation metadata gates.
Customer Portal modules are inserted additively without replacing existing workflows.
The app-specific subscription webhooks are released on API version 2026-07.
The delivery scheduler, idempotent dispatcher, acknowledgement path, monitoring, and storage signer are active.
The full controlled end-to-end verification suite passes.
Publication receives a distinct executive approval bound to the exact release commit.

integration_sequence.current_component: Live Shopify provisioning and end-to-end deployment control
integration_sequence.next_component: Operational monitoring, incident response, and controlled production rollout
